title: Customers Above Average Order Count
description: Identify customers who have placed more orders than the average number of orders per customer. Return the customer name along with their total number of orders.
sql_solution: WITH counts AS (SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) cnt FROM orders GROUP BY customer_id), avg_val AS (SELECT AVG(cnt) avg_cnt FROM counts) SELECT c.name, cnt FROM counts JOIN customers c ON counts.customer_id = c.id WHERE cnt > (SELECT avg_cnt FROM avg_val);
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *

customer_counts = orders.groupBy("customer_id").agg(count(lit(1)).alias("cnt"))
average_count = customer_counts.agg(avg("cnt").alias("avg_cnt"))

result = (
    customer_counts.alias("cc")
    .join(customers.alias("c"), col("cc.customer_id") == col("c.id"), "inner")
    .crossJoin(average_count)
    .filter(col("cc.cnt") > col("avg_cnt"))
    .select(col("c.name").alias("name"), col("cc.cnt").alias("cnt"))
)
